summary: Map awards/rookie, pro-rel, agent-pool/role-eligibility, off-role fit, and benching/happiness systems; design concrete fixes for benching, rookie-multi-win, pro/rel, tier-1 awards, agent flex, and compatibility-based off-role fit

plan.summary: Six requests turned into one build-incrementable plan, ordered correctness-first then features. Verified against live code: the ROTY gate at GameManager.cpp:3404 is exactly `career_seasons_played <= 1 && season_rating(*p) >= 0.90` with no has-won guard (real bug, double-win confirmed); tier-1 award filter is already correct (verify-only); the agent-pool size roll (Player.cpp:3368-3377) and the off-role 0.85 flat penalty (Team.cpp:1454, 1369-1371 role-filter) match the maps. Order: (1) ROTY once-ever fix, (2) tier-1 verify, (3) off-role compatibility penalty + agent-eligibility loosening, (4) VCT-accurate agent pools, (5) benching feature, (6) unhappiness/transfer-request, (7) pro/rel improvements last (largest, lowest correctness-urgency). Two smoke-test bands will shift: the flat-0.85 off-role assertion and the agent-pool-size distribution histogram. Never-free invariant preserved throughout (all roster/league moves are pointer swaps/reorders; no frees).

items.request: Fix Rookie of the Year won multiple times (must be once-ever)
items.verdict: CONFIRMED BUG. Verified live at GameManager.cpp:3403-3405: gate is `career_seasons_played <= 1 && season_rating(*p) >= 0.90` with NO has-won guard. Because career_seasons_played is incremented later (Player.cpp:1313, during save_history_and_progress which runs at callsite 1575, AFTER compute_season_awards at 1494), a true debut player hits awards at count==0, wins, bumps to 1; next season hits at count==1, still satisfies <=1, wins AGAIN. The per-year award-pin dedupe at 3237-3239 embeds the year in award_str so it never blocks a cross-year repeat. Two independent defects: off-by-one window AND missing once-ever guard.
steps: Edit the ROTY gate lambda body at GameManager.cpp:3403-3405. Prepend an awards-scan that hard-excludes any prior winner: `for (auto& a : p->awards) if (a.find("Rookie of the Year") != std::string::npos) return false;`. This substring-matches the pinned string `[A] Rookie of the Year <year>` (built at GameManager.cpp:3236) across ALL years, so a previous winner is permanently excluded regardless of the counter timing. This single edit alone satisfies the once-ever requirement.

Also tighten the rookie window: change `career_seasons_played <= 1` to `career_seasons_played == 0` at GameManager.cpp:3404, so ROTY means strictly the debut season (the counter is pre-increment at award time, so a genuine first-year pro is 0). Apply BOTH the scan and `==0` (scan = hard once-ever guarantee, `==0` = correct semantics).

Verify p->awards is the vector the pin at GameManager.cpp:3236-3239 writes to (it is the Player::awards member referenced by the generic pin builder); confirm the scan reads that same vector so a win recorded this year blocks next year.
Build the cpp project per Valosim CLAUDE.md build command (the D:/MinecraftServer path in env is unrelated).
Simulate 2+ seasons and confirm a prior ROTY winner never reappears in last_season_awards for the ROTY category, and that a genuine debut player can still win once.
items.risk: Very low. Pure gate tightening; cannot leak players into the pool (only removes them). The only behavioral question is the deliberate window: if a 2-year rookie window is desired, keep `<=1` but the scan still guarantees at-most-once. Recommended `==0` + scan is the cleanest. No never-free impact.
items.test: Add/extend smoke_test.cpp: build a player, run compute_season_awards twice across two simulated seasons with season_rating>=0.90 both years; assert the player appears in the ROTY award exactly once and that p->awards contains exactly one 'Rookie of the Year' entry. No existing band shifts.

items.request: Keep MVP + role/IGL awards TIER-1 ONLY (verify/ensure)
items.verdict: ALREADY CORRECT — verify only, no code change. The single tier1_players set (GameManager.cpp:3122-3124) is filled by walking ONLY `leagues` (tier-1 VCT). The shared `qualified` pool gates every candidate via `if (!tier1_players.count(p.get())) continue;` (3130) plus `season_matches < 8` (3129). MVP, all four Role-of-the-Year, IGL, ROTY, and Most Improved all consume this one `qualified` vector through add_award's loop (3189). IGL adds extra guards (not retired, not FA/Retired, igl_match_count>0) at 3382-3393. No award re-adds from solo_qs or tier_leagues_. No tier-2/Challengers leak exists.
steps: Confirm by inspection that tier_leagues_ tiers 1 and 2 (Challengers/Open Circuit) are never added to tier1_players (only `leagues` is iterated at GameManager.cpp:3122-3124).
Confirm every add_award call site draws from the same `qualified` vector (GameManager.cpp:3189) and none bypass the 3130 filter.

No edit required. Document the invariant with a one-line comment above GameManager.cpp:3121 (`// tier-1 ONLY: every award below consumes `qualified`, gated by tier1_players`) so future awards inherit the guard intentionally.
Optional hardening note: the per-year pin dedupe (3237-3239) is NOT a cross-year once-ever guard; any future once-ever award must add its own awards-scan exactly like the ROTY fix.
items.risk: None — verification task. Adding only a comment carries zero behavioral risk.
items.test: Add a smoke_test.cpp assertion that constructs a tier-2/Challengers player with an MVP-caliber season_rating and runs compute_season_awards, asserting that player wins NO tier-1 award (MVP/role/IGL). Locks in the tier-1-only invariant against regression.

items.request: Make off-role FIT in map strategy compatibility-driven (smooth role_fit_score penalty, not flat 0.85)
items.verdict: CONFIRMED FLAT/BINARY today. Verified at Team.cpp:1454 `if (best_off_role) rating *= 0.85;` — same 15% haircut for an 0.84-compat near-fit and a 0.30-compat mismatch. best_off_role is a pool-wide boolean set at 1373 (true only when the player had NO on-role pooled agent), never reads role_fit_score. The override path mirrors this with flat +25 (Team.cpp:1285) and flat *0.85 (1292). role_fit_score (Player.cpp:161) and role_fit_verdict (201) already exist and are unused by build_round_selection. The resign path (Player.cpp:3038-3054) uses role_fit_score but via discontinuous step-bands (8-point cliff at rf=0.75).
steps: Add a shared smooth helper near role_fit_score's declaration (~Player.h:567): `inline double role_fit_penalty_mult(double rf){ if(rf>=1.0) return 1.0; double m = 0.78 + 0.22*rf; return m<0.78?0.78:(m>1.0?1.0:m); }`. Maps rf=0.80->0.956 (small penalty), rf=0.30->0.846, rf=0.0->0.78. Smooth, monotonic.

Auto-picker realized penalty: at Team.cpp:1451 after best_a is chosen, compute `double best_fit = p->role_fit_score(best_a->role);`. Replace lines 1453-1454 (`double rating = best_rating_int; if (best_off_role) rating *= 0.85;`) with `double rating = best_rating_int * role_fit_penalty_mult(best_fit);`. role_fit_score returns 1.00 for primary so on-role auto-yields mult 1.0; best_off_role plumbing can stay for UI but no longer drives the haircut.

Override realized penalty: replace Team.cpp:1292 `if (ps[bi]->primary_role != a->role) rr *= 0.85;` with `rr *= role_fit_penalty_mult(ps[bi]->role_fit_score(a->role));` (no branch needed; primary yields 1.0).

Override selection bonus: replace Team.cpp:1285 `if (ps[i]->primary_role == a->role) r += 25.0;` with `r += 25.0 * ps[i]->role_fit_score(a->role);` so the picker prefers high-compat flexes over low-compat ones.

Resign role_mod (Player.cpp:3038-3054): replace the 4-step ladder with `double rf = role_fit_score(r); role_mod = (rf>=1.0)?0:-(int)std::lround((1.0 - rf) * 50.0);` and preserve the reject guard `if (rf < 0.35 && b.reject_reason.empty()) b.reject_reason = ...;`. Audit the `role_mod > -40` reachable gate at Player.cpp:3122 (now -40 occurs at rf=0.20, still only genuine mismatches) and the personality_only subtraction at 3167 (unchanged, role_mod still single folded term).

Note: to actually let a compatible player be slotted onto an off-role agent at all, this item depends on loosening the agent-eligibility filter (see step in next item / the build_round_selection role-filter at Team.cpp:1369-1371). The smooth penalty governs the COST once off-role is reachable; the filter governs whether it is reachable. Sequence: loosen filter, then apply smooth penalty.
Build the cpp project.
items.risk: Medium. Lowering effective off-role cost makes the AI picker and resign logic more willing to flex players; team_ovr will no longer collapse on off-role, which is intended but shifts match-balance slightly. The `role_mod > -40` reachable threshold semantics move from the 0.35 band to rf=0.20 — verify that reads as intended. Keep the floor at 0.78 so a true mismatch stays meaningfully worse. Never-free unaffected.
items.test: BAND WILL SHIFT. Add/extend smoke_test.cpp near the resign tests (~1290) and build_round_selection tests (~1428-1486): assert monotonicity (higher role_fit_score never yields a worse role_mod nor a smaller selection mult), and assert an 80%-compat off-role pick keeps >0.94 of its rating while a 30%-compat pick keeps <0.87. The OLD flat-0.85 assertion (any off-role == 0.85) must be REPLACED — flag this band as intentionally changed.

items.request: Make the agent system VCT-accurate (players too strict — most pros play role + 2-3 off-role agents; keep rare one-tricks)
items.verdict: CONFIRMED TOO STRICT. Verified the size roll at Player.cpp:3368-3377: 55% of pros get size 3, 10% get 1-2, only ~35% reach 4+. update_agent_pool (Player.cpp:562-646) pins the best primary-role agent (630-632) then fills from the global score-sorted list where every primary-role agent gets +6.0 (Player.cpp:583), so a size-3 pro is effectively single-role with ~0 cross-role agents. The off-role data exists but the small cap + the +6.0 steer crowd it out. Consumption (Team.cpp:1369-1375) role-filters the pool, so a thin pool literally blocks cross-role slotting.
steps: Widen the size roll at Player.cpp:3368-3377. Replace the ladder with: `if (r < 0.03) sz = rng().irange(1,2); else if (r < 0.15) sz = 3; else if (r < 0.45) sz = 4; else if (r < 0.79) sz = 5; else if (r < 0.97) sz = 6; else sz = 7;` Shifts modal pro from 3 to 5; one-tricks ~6%, ultra-flex (7) ~3%. Update the comment block at Player.cpp:3359-3367 to match.
Add `int cross_role_min = 2;` to Player.h near agent_pool_size (line 347).

In update_agent_pool, after pinning primary_top (Player.cpp:632) and BEFORE the generic Step-2 fill (641), insert a reserved off-role phase: `int kCross = (agent_pool_size <= 2) ? 0 : std::min(cross_role_min, agent_pool_size-1);` then walk the score-sorted `all` vector pushing the best kCross agents whose role != primary_role (skip already-pinned). Then run the existing Step-2 loop to fill remaining slots. This guarantees a size-5 pro has >=2 off-role agents while keeping size<=2 as pure one-tricks.

Soften the steer: reduce the primary-role bonus at Player.cpp:583 from +6.0 to +3.0 so genuinely-high off-role fits compete for non-reserved slots organically (primary main still wins via pinning). Update the explanatory comment at Player.cpp:570-576.

Tie breadth to flex: at the FlexibleUtility bump (Player.cpp:1924) and for high-Adaptability players, set cross_role_min=3 so league flex players reliably cover 3 roles. Revisit the FlexGenius gate at Player.cpp:2303 (currently pool>=4) — with modal size now 5 it triggers far more often; either accept or raise its Adaptability threshold.
Build the cpp project.
items.risk: Medium-high (broad league effect). Wider pools + guaranteed cross-role coverage changes who can be slotted off-role league-wide, interacting with item 3 (off-role penalty) and item 5 (benching). Watch that one-tricks stay ~5-8% and ultra-flex stays rare (~0.1-3%). The reduced +6.0->+3.0 steer must not let an off-role agent out-pin the primary main (the explicit pin at 632 prevents this). Never-free unaffected (pool is vector<const Agent*>, no ownership).
items.test: BAND WILL SHIFT. Add a smoke_test.cpp distribution dump: generate ~2000 players via generate_player, histogram agent_pool.size() and the count of off-role agents (role != primary_role). Assert median total pool == 5, >=80% of non-one-trick players have >=2 off-role agents, one-tricks (<=1 off-role) stay 5-8%, size-7 stays 0.1-3%. Any existing assertion expecting modal size 3 or ~0 cross-role agents must be updated — flag this band.

items.request: BENCHING feature (bench bad/contract-stuck players; player stays paid)
items.verdict: MISSING — must build. Verified there is NO is_starter flag anywhere; the starting 5 is literally roster[0..4] of Team::roster (Team.h:269, cap 7). The match engine slices roster.begin()..roster.begin()+5 (Team.cpp:1276/1313); enforce_one_igl (537) and enforce_one_flex (666) both scope to min(5,roster.size()); the GUI DrawRoster derives buckets every frame from vector order (gui_main.cpp:5772-5804). So benching == reordering the vector — every consumer already reads 'first 5 slots'. A flag would require editing all of them. Also found a latent bug: release_player (Team.cpp:711) does NOT re-run enforce_one_igl/flex after erase() shifts a bench player into the 5.
steps: Add `bool Team::swap_roster_slots(const PlayerPtr& benchee, const PlayerPtr& starter);` declaration to Team.h after release_player (~Team.h:286). Implement in Team.cpp: locate both via std::find; require one index in [0,4] and the other in [5,roster.size()); std::iter_swap them; then call enforce_one_igl() + enforce_one_flex() (mirroring sign_player Team.cpp:461-462) so IGL stat-shift and Flex overlay recompute for the new 5. Return false if either not found or both on the same side. Player stays rostered + paid (total_payroll_k Team.cpp:724 sums whole roster) — matches 'still under contract'.
Fix the latent enforcer bug: add enforce_one_igl()+enforce_one_flex() at the end of release_player (after Team.cpp:721) since erase() can promote a bench player into the 5 without re-electing an IGL.

GUI: extend the render_row lambda (gui_main.cpp:5813) with a bench/starter context flag. Add a SmallButton('Bench') in the STARTERS rows (~gui_main.cpp:5972-5981) and a SmallButton('Start') in the BENCH rows (~6006-6008). Gate 'Start' to enable only when roster.size()>=5.

For 'Start', auto-pick the same-Position starter to displace using vlr::position_of against the canonical[idx] occupant from the bucketing block (gui_main.cpp:5782), so it is one-click position-preserving (1D+1C+1I+1S+flex shape stays intact). Optional secondary path: a popup listing the 5 starters.

Wire through a deferred action list like to_release (gui_main.cpp:5806, applied at 6014): collect std::vector<std::pair<PlayerPtr,PlayerPtr>> to_swap during render, apply AFTER EndTable via t->swap_roster_slots(benchee, starter), push an s.log line. Deferral avoids mutating roster mid-iteration.
Leave the opponent Team Profile view (gui_main.cpp:8589-8612) read-only — expose controls only on the user's DrawRoster, matching Release/Re-sign.

Verify the save/load serializer writes roster in vector order (grep the serializer for `roster`); if it sorts/rebuilds on load, add an explicit per-player order index to the save format so the manual bench arrangement survives. If order is already preserved, no engine read-path change is needed.
Build the cpp project.
items.risk: Medium. The reorder approach is low-risk for the engine (all consumers read order), but the IGL stat-shift must revert correctly when the IGL is benched (apply_igl_stat_shift(...,-1) via enforce_one_igl) or stats drift. Save/load order preservation is the main correctness risk — verify before shipping. Never-free preserved: benching is iter_swap, release is the existing erase (no new frees; player object stays owned by roster or moves to FA).
items.test: Add smoke_test.cpp: build a 7-player roster with the starting-5 IGL; call swap_roster_slots to bench the IGL for a bench player; assert enforce_one_igl re-elected an IGL among the new roster[0..4], the demoted IGL's stat shift reverted, build_round_selection now fields the new 5, and total_payroll_k is unchanged (player still paid). Also test the release_player enforcer fix: release a starter and assert the promoted bench player gets a valid IGL/Flex overlay.

items.request: Unhappy player can force a move off the team (agitation / transfer request)
items.verdict: MISSING — must build. Verified happiness today is ONLY FreeAgentMood (Player.h:286-288/422), a per-org 0..1 grievance that merely inflates salary demands (amount_with_mood, Player.cpp:508) and ghosts lowballs (refuses_to_negotiate, 513). A maxed mood=1.0 player still sits forever; nothing pushes them off or signals the market. No wants_out/transfer_request/disgruntled flag exists (grep = 0 hits). The poach auction (GameManager.cpp:2010-2155) only sees EXPIRING contracts, never unhappy ones. Mood drivers today are only benched-promise (Player.cpp:1283-1286) and lowball re-sign (3194-3198).
steps: Add state to Player.h near mood (~422): `double discontent = 0.0;`, `bool transfer_requested = false;`, `int transfer_request_year = 0;`. Keep FreeAgentMood untouched (per-org negotiation memory); discontent is the NEW global drives-a-move signal.

Add discontent drivers in Player::save_history_and_progress (Player.cpp:1283, BEFORE counters reset at 1381), extending the existing benched-promise block: (a) benched-promise -> discontent += 0.35 when promised_starter && season_matches==0; (b) low playtime graded -> if season_matches<8 add 0.15, if <4 add another 0.10, scaled by ego (Player.h:325): `discontent += base*(0.7+0.6*ego/99.0)`; (c) losing -> use the team_placement param already in the signature (Player.cpp:1271) to add 0.10 for bottom-half/relegation, scaled by ambition (Player.h:322).

Add decay + threshold in the same function: decay last year first (discontent *= 0.6 so a good season heals), clamp [0,1], then `if (discontent >= 0.6 && !transfer_requested && team_name not FA/Retired){ transfer_requested = true; transfer_request_year = year; }`. Clear `transfer_requested` when discontent < 0.3.

Push news when a request trips: in the GameManager year-end sweep (after save_history_and_progress per player, near GameManager.cpp:1573-1584), if p->transfer_requested && p->transfer_request_year==year, push a NewsItem via the existing push_news pattern (GameManager.cpp:1862-1893): '<player> hands in a transfer request at <team>'.

Market reaction (AI teams only): add a pre-pass over each AI team roster before/alongside the FA-poach auction (GameManager.cpp:2010-2155). For each p with transfer_requested, run the same candidate-gather + bid loop (2032-2110) at a DISCOUNTED floor: where floor_budget is computed (2018-2020) apply `floor *= (1.0 - 0.30*p->discontent)`. If a bid clears, prev_team->release_player(p) then winner->sign_player (mirror 2109-2117); if discontent>=0.85 and no bid, release to FA anyway. Exclude user_team (parity with existing `if (other==user_team) continue`).

User-team path: never auto-manage the user's roster. Surface transfer_requested as a badge + 'Listen to offers / Release' action in the GUI player view (gui_main.cpp roster render, alongside mood/contract), and let AI bids for the user's flagged players appear as acceptable offers in the OFFSEASON phase (GameManager.cpp:888-941). Preserves the never-touch-user-team invariant while letting an unhappy player force the user's hand.

Clear on resolution: when re-signed with an honored starter promise, sold, or after a full winning season, drop discontent and clear transfer_requested (the *0.6 decay plus explicit reset at sign_player/resign_player and on release).
Build the cpp project.
items.risk: Medium-high. The new auto-sell pre-pass mutates AI rosters mid-offseason — sequence it carefully against the existing re-sign/poach pass (GameManager.cpp:1728-2155) and run release_player BEFORE sign_player to keep counts sane. Must exclude user_team everywhere. Discontent tuning (thresholds/decay) needs simulation to avoid mass churn. Never-free preserved: release_player sets team_name='Free Agent' and erases from roster but the player object lives in the FA pool (existing pattern, no free).
items.test: Add smoke_test.cpp (file already references mood): (a) set promised_starter+promise_active, run save_history_and_progress with season_matches==0 for ~2 years, assert transfer_requested flips true; (b) run a winning full season, assert it clears (discontent<0.3); (c) drive the GameManager offseason and assert an AI-team agitator is sold or released and appears at a discounted floor (floor reduced by ~0.30*discontent).

items.verdict: WORKS but UNREALISTIC/INCOMPLETE — enhancement, not a bug. Verified the driver resolve_promotion_relegation (GameManager.cpp:4649-4732) moves exactly ONE team per boundary per year via Ascension (top-6 by raw ->wins, single-elim) vs a relegation gauntlet against hi_sorted.back() (the single worst team by raw wins, no tiebreak). apply_promotion/apply_relegation (4624-4647) only bump budget/prestige — NO roster change, so a <=62-ovr-capped T2 team enters the 12-team VCT as-is. kTiers counts are 12/10/12 (asymmetric funnel). Swaps are pure pointer moves (never-free OK). Placed LAST because it is the largest change with the least correctness urgency.
steps: Standings-based relegation with tiebreakers: at GameManager.cpp:4686-4691 replace the single ->wins sort + back() with a comparator over (wins, then map/round differential, then head-to-head). Add a Team season map_diff field if absent and accumulate it in play_tier_stage_round (4574-4594) and play_stage_round. Take bottom-K (configurable, e.g. 2) instead of just 1 so a bad team cannot escape on one lucky bo5.

Multi-team Ascension: add `int promote_slots` to TierConfig in kTiers (GameManager.cpp:4419-4429) and have run_bracket_to_completion (4596) return top-N finishers, not just the champ. Update Phase 1 (4667-4705) to build a list of (promo,releg) pairs per boundary and Phase 2 (4708-4726) to apply each. Keep counts neutral by pairing each promotion with a relegation (preserves per-league count invariant in apply_promotion/relegation).

Partner-slot (no-relegation) mode for the T1/T2 boundary only: branch on u==1 inside resolve_promotion_relegation (4667). Promote the Ascension champ into VCT and, instead of forced relegation, either grow the VCT league or relegate on a 2-year rolling worst-record rule (mirrors real VCT). Keep the gauntlet for T2/T3 where lower divisions do relegate.

Roster handling on move: in apply_promotion (GameManager.cpp:4624-4635) after the pointer move, run a budget-scaled GM pass (ai_full_offseason_pass against the region FA pool, or raise the roster's effective ovr cap) so a promoted org upgrades toward VCT quality. Symmetrically in apply_relegation (4637-4647) optionally let high-ovr players want out (set transfer_requested from item 6, or team_name='Free Agent') so a relegated team loses stars. Sequence BEFORE run_lower_tier_offseason re-fills (callsites 2205/2284).

Fix the funnel: change kTiers counts (GameManager.cpp:4426-4428) to a believable pyramid (e.g. T1=12, T2=12, T3=16) OR keep promote/relegate slots equal so paired swaps stay count-neutral; document the conservation invariant in apply_promotion/apply_relegation.

Surface multi-team results: PromoRelResult.promoted/relegated (GameManager.h:182-185) are already vectors — populate them with ALL moved teams; store ascension_champion per boundary (currently only u==1 at 4683) as a vector/map keyed by tier; update the pro/rel UI (~gui_main.cpp:8477) to list every promotion/relegation.
Build the cpp project.
items.risk: High (largest surface, multi-function). Roster rebuild on promotion interacts with the offseason loop ordering (resolve_promotion_relegation runs at GameManager.cpp:2205 BEFORE the tier-1 wins-reset at 2211 and before run_lower_tier_offseason at 2284) — preserve that ordering or Ascension seeding breaks. Multi-team swaps must keep per-league counts conserved (pair every up with a down) or leagues drift from kTiers sizes. The map_diff accumulation must be reset each season (alongside the wins reset at 4790). Never-free preserved as long as all moves stay pointer erase+push_back (the existing pattern, comment at 2199-2204).
items.test: Add smoke_test.cpp: simulate a full year-end pro/rel pass and assert (a) per-league team counts match kTiers after all swaps (count-neutral invariant), (b) the relegation comparator picks the team with worst (wins,map_diff) not just worst wins, (c) a promoted team's roster ovr increased after the budget-scaled GM pass, (d) PromoRelResult.promoted/relegated list every moved team. Verify no league pointer is freed (never-free).

maps.component: compute_season_awards in C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/src/GameManager.cpp (Rookie of the Year gate + tier-1 qualification filter)
current_behavior: AWARD ORDER: run_end_of_year calls compute_season_awards FIRST (C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/src/GameManager.cpp:1494), then save_history_and_progress runs later in the same year-end pass (GameManager.cpp:1575).

career_seasons_played is ONLY incremented inside Player::save_history_and_progress, at C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/src/Player.cpp:1313: `if (season_matches >= 8) ++career_seasons_played;`. Declared default 0 at C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/include/Player.h:443.
Therefore at award time the counter holds the value from the START of the season (the increment for the just-finished season has NOT happened yet).

ROTY gate (GameManager.cpp:3401-3406): `return p->career_seasons_played <= 1 && season_rating(*p) >= 0.90;`. A true debut player reaches awards with career_seasons_played==0, wins, then it bumps to 1. The NEXT season that same player reaches awards with career_seasons_played==1, which still satisfies <=1, so they win ROTY a SECOND time, then it bumps to 2. So `<=1` grants every player two ROTY-eligible seasons.
There is NO 'already won' guard anywhere — Grep for 'Rookie of the Year' / 'has_won' / '[A]' returns only the gate (3401), the comment (3397), and the generic pin builder (3236).

The award-pin dedupe at GameManager.cpp:3237-3239 only checks for an identical award_str, and award_str embeds the YEAR (`[A] Rookie of the Year 2026`), so it never blocks the same category won in a DIFFERENT year.

TIER-1 FILTER (MVP/role/IGL): the qualified pool is built at GameManager.cpp:3121-3133. tier1_players is filled by walking ONLY `leagues` (tier-1; GameManager.cpp:3122-3124), and every candidate must pass `if (!tier1_players.count(p.get())) continue;` (GameManager.cpp:3130) plus `season_matches < 8` (3129). MVP, all four Role-of-the-Year, IGL, ROTY and Most Improved all draw exclusively from this `qualified` vector via add_award's loop (3189). No award re-adds players from solo_qs or tier_leagues_ outside this filter, so no tier-2/Challengers player can win. current_behavior: Pyramid storage: tier_leagues_ maps region -> [T1,T2,T3] (GameManager.h:176). Index 0 ALIASES the same LeaguePtr as leagues[region] (the tier-1 VCT league); tiers 1-based, index = tier-1. tier_count/league_at/league_for/tier_of scan this map (GameManager.cpp:4455-4482).

Note the count MISMATCH: T1=12, T2=10, T3=12.

In-season: play_tier_stage_round (GameManager.cpp:4574-4594) plays lower tiers each week using a SEPARATE cursor t2_week (GameManager.h:177), bo3 league-play series that record wins/phase_wins, plus a flat +5000 stage stipend per team. Tier-1 is played separately by play_stage_round.

Year-end driver: run_end_of_year calls resolve_promotion_relegation(log) (GameManager.cpp:2205) BEFORE the tier-1 rebuild loop, because that loop resets wins to 0 (2211) and Ascension seeding reads season wins. Comment at 2199-2204 stresses swaps are pure pointer moves so the never-free invariant holds.

resolve_promotion_relegation (GameManager.cpp:4649-4732): for each region, for each adjacent boundary u (upper tier u vs lower tier u+1): Phase 1 decides every boundary on PRE-swap standings. Ascension = take lower division's teams, sort DESC by ->wins, take top kAscField=6 (4651,4677-4679), run_bracket_to_completion single-elim bracket -> one champ. Relegation gauntlet = sort higher division DESC by wins, take hi_sorted.back() = the single WORST team (4691); play one bo5 worst-vs-champ (4693). If challenger wins (sign<0) queue a Swap{champ,worst,u}; else champ 'stays put' (news at 4699).

Phase 2 (GameManager.cpp:4708-4726): for each queued swap, apply_relegation(worst,u) then apply_promotion(champ,u). Exactly ONE team can move each direction per boundary per year. With 3 tiers there are 2 boundaries (T1/T2 and T2/T3), so at most 1 team promoted + 1 relegated per boundary per region.

apply_promotion (GameManager.cpp:4624-4635): erase t from lower league teams(), push_back into higher league teams(); +400000 budget, +12 prestige (clamp 1-99), recompute sponsorship. apply_relegation (4637-4647): erase from higher, push into lower; -200000 budget (floored at 0), -12 prestige. Region never changes. Team-count is conserved at each boundary ONLY because every swap is paired (one up, one down); if a gauntlet is not won, no movement, counts unchanged.

Roster on move: NOTHING happens to the roster during the move — apply_promotion/relegation only touch budget/prestige/sponsorship. The promoted team keeps its ovr-capped (<=62 for T2) roster and is dropped into the 12-team VCT league as-is. Offseason maintenance happens AFTER in the normal loops.

Offseason after swaps: tier-1 loop (GameManager.cpp:2207-2276) runs ai_full_offseason_pass on every T1 team (the promoted team now lives here, so it gets a VCT-budget GM pass), resets wins, regenerates schedule. run_lower_tier_offseason (4734-4809, called at 2284) does the same for T2/T3: re-sign useful expiring players, release retired/passed, reset wins, ai_full_offseason_pass, regenerate schedule. Wins reset here (4790) so next year's Ascension seeds on fresh season wins (the B2 fix).
last_promo_rel_ (PromoRelResult) records promoted/relegated/ascension_champion per region for UI/news; only u==1 (T1/T2) champ is stored as ascension_champion (GameManager.cpp:4683).

change_plan: WHAT IS INCOMPLETE / UNREALISTIC vs real VCT: (a) Only 1 team moves per boundary per year — real VCT Ascension promotes ONE team but to a FIXED partner league of ~11 international slots, and there is effectively NO annual relegation of partner teams (the swap model here invents a relegation that VCT does not have). (b) Relegation target is hi_sorted.back() = single worst team by raw ->wins with NO tiebreak (map differential / head-to-head), and 'worst' is decided on regular-season wins ignoring playoffs. (c) Ascension field is top-6 by wins with the same crude tiebreak; real Ascension is a multi-region LAN won through a bracket, here it is a per-region top-6 single-elim. (d) Promoted team carries its <=62-ovr-capped roster straight into a 12-team VCT league with only a flat +400k/+12 prestige — no roster rebuild, so it is unrealistically weak its first VCT year. (e) Team-count asymmetry: T1=12, T2=10, T3=12 (kTiers:4426-4428) means swaps keep each league's own count constant but the pyramid is not a clean funnel. (f) No standings-based auto-relegation as an alternative to the gauntlet; the bottom team can survive forever by winning one bo5.

IMPROVEMENT 1 — standings-based relegation with real tiebreakers. In resolve_promotion_relegation (GameManager.cpp:4686-4691) replace the single ->wins sort + back() with a proper standings comparator: sort by (wins, then map/round differential, then head-to-head). Add a Team field for season map_diff if absent and accumulate it in play_tier_stage_round/play_stage_round. Then take the bottom-K (configurable, e.g. 2) as relegation candidates rather than just 1, so a genuinely bad team cannot escape on one lucky bo5.

IMPROVEMENT 2 — multi-team Ascension. Add a per-boundary promotion-slot count to TierConfig (e.g. add int promote_slots to kTiers @ GameManager.cpp:4419-4429) and have run_bracket_to_completion return the top-N finishers, not just the champion. Promote N teams and relegate the symmetric bottom-N from the upper tier (or, to mirror real VCT, promote N with NO forced relegation and instead expand/contract the upper league — guarded by keeping team-count invariants explicit). Update Phase 1 loop (4667-4705) to build a list of (promo,releg) pairs per boundary and Phase 2 (4708-4726) to apply each.

IMPROVEMENT 3 — partner-slot (no-relegation) mode for the T1/T2 boundary only. Gate the T1/T2 boundary (u==1) to pure Ascension promotion: promote the Ascension champ into VCT and, instead of relegating, either grow the VCT league past 12 or relegate strictly on a 2-year rolling worst-record rule. Keep the gauntlet model for the T2/T3 boundary where real-life lower divisions DO relegate. Branch on u inside resolve_promotion_relegation (4667).

IMPROVEMENT 4 — roster handling on move. In apply_promotion (GameManager.cpp:4624-4635) after the pointer move, run a budget-scaled GM pass so the promoted org actually upgrades toward VCT quality (call ai_full_offseason_pass against the region FA pool, or at least raise its roster's effective ovr cap) instead of only bumping budget/prestige. Symmetrically in apply_relegation (4637-4647) optionally let stars leave (mark high-ovr players as wanting out / set their team_name to Free Agent) so a relegated team realistically loses talent. Sequence this so it runs BEFORE run_lower_tier_offseason re-fills (callsite 2205/2284).

IMPROVEMENT 5 — fix tier-count funnel. Change kTiers (GameManager.cpp:4426-4428) so counts form a believable pyramid (e.g. T1=12, T2=12, T3=16) or make promote/relegate slots equal so swaps stay count-neutral; document the invariant in apply_promotion/apply_relegation that paired swaps conserve per-league counts.

IMPROVEMENT 6 — surface multi-team results. Extend PromoRelResult (GameManager.h:182-185) promoted/relegated already are vectors, so populate them with all moved teams; store ascension_champion per boundary (currently only u==1, GameManager.cpp:4683) e.g. as a vector or map keyed by tier, and update the gui_main.cpp pro/rel UI (around gui_main.cpp:8477) to list every promotion/relegation rather than a single champion.

current_behavior: POOL SIZE is rolled once at spawn in generate_player(): C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/src/Player.cpp:3368-3377. Distribution: r<0.001 -> 6-7 (ultra flex, ~0.1%); r<0.10 -> 5 (~9.9%); r<0.35 -> 4 (~25%); r<0.90 -> 3 (~55%); else -> 1-2 (~10%). Default member value is 3 (Player.h:347).

The pool itself is BUILT by Player::update_agent_pool() at C:/Users/fulls/Desktop/Valosim/cpp/src/Player.cpp:562-646. Step 1 (line 630-632) ALWAYS pins the single best primary-role agent first. Step 2 (line 634-645) fills the remaining slots up to agent_pool_size from the global score-sorted list across ANY role, skipping the pinned one. target = clamp(agent_pool_size, 1, AG.size()) at line 640.

Agent fit score is base = 0.45*v1 + 0.32*v2 + 0.23*v3 over the agent's three identity attributes, PLUS a +6.0 bonus if a.role == primary_role, PLUS up to +12 mastery (Player.cpp:577-586, comment 568-572). The +6.0 role steer is described as 'modest' but is decisive at small pool sizes.

primary_role is NOT an input to attribute generation — it is DERIVED after the fact. generate_player fills every attribute from one shared 'base' mean with small per-attr spread (Player.cpp:3253-3281, fill() at 3254-3256). update_agent_pool then picks primary_role as the role whose top-2 raw-fit agents sum highest (Player.cpp:594-612). So a player has no systematic attribute deficit off-role; off-role agents lose pool slots almost entirely because of the +6.0 role bonus combined with the tiny pool cap, not because they fail on attributes.

NET EFFECT for the 55% majority (size 3): slot 1 = pinned best primary-role agent; slots 2-3 = next two highest global scorers, which — because every primary-role agent gets the +6.0 — are almost always ALSO primary-role agents. A size-3 pro is effectively a single-role player with 0 cross-role agents. The ~10% at size 1-2 are even narrower. Only size 4+ (~35%) start spilling into a second role, and even then off-role agents must out-score on-role agents despite a 6.0 handicap.

Roster math (Agent.cpp:29-66): 29 agents = 8 Duelist, 7 Controller, 7 Initiator, 7 Sentinel. With +6.0 on, the 6-8 same-role agents dominate the sorted list, so for size<=3 the off-role count is essentially 0.

Archetype overrides exist but are narrow: FlexibleUtility rookie sets agent_pool_size = max(size,5) (Player.cpp:1924), and FlexGenius archetype only triggers when Adaptability>=72 AND pool>=4 (Player.cpp:2303). Neither widens the typical pro.

CONSUMPTION confirms the pool is the eligibility gate: Team.cpp:1369-1371 only considers agent_pool entries matching the target role; only if that yields nothing (off_role fallback, line 1372-1375) does it open up to all agents of that role. So a thin pool literally restricts who can be slotted onto cross-role/flex duty (Team.cpp:1338-1349 flex hint uses agent_pool.back()).

change_plan: GOAL (concrete target distribution): a typical pro should carry their primary role's main agent + 1-2 secondary same-role agents + 2-3 cross-role agents = ~5-6 total agents, of which at least 2 are off-role. Keep rare one-tricks (~5-8%) and rare ultra-flex unicorns (~0.1%). The fix has TWO independent levers: (a) raise pool SIZE, and (b) GUARANTEE a minimum off-role count so size alone doesn't get eaten by same-role agents via the +6.0 bonus.

STEP 1 — widen the size roll at Player.cpp:3368-3377. Replace the current ladder. CURRENT vs PROPOSED probability mass: size 1-2 one-trick 10% -> 6%; size 3 55% -> 12%; size 4 25% -> 30%; size 5 9.9% -> 34%; size 6 ~0% -> 15%; size 7 0.1% -> 3% (ultra unicorn). Concretely: 'if (r < 0.03) sz = rng().irange(1,2); else if (r < 0.15) sz = 3; else if (r < 0.45) sz = 4; else if (r < 0.79) sz = 5; else if (r < 0.97) sz = 6; else sz = 7;'. This shifts the modal pro from 3 to 5 and makes 4-6 the common band.

STEP 2 — add a member 'int cross_role_min' (default 2) to Player.h near line 347, OR reuse a local. Then GUARANTEE off-role coverage in update_agent_pool() so the +6.0 same-role bonus can't crowd out every cross-role agent. After Step 1 pins primary_top (Player.cpp:632) and BEFORE the generic fill loop (Player.cpp:641), insert a reserved phase: walk the score-sorted 'all' vector and push the best K agents whose role != primary_role (K = std::min(cross_role_min, agent_pool_size-1)), so e.g. a size-5 pro is guaranteed >=2 off-role agents. Then run the existing Step-2 loop to fill any leftover slots from the global order. This directly delivers 'role + 2-3 cross-role agents'.

STEP 3 — soften the steer so cross-role picks are organic, not just forced. Reduce the primary-role bonus at Player.cpp:583 from +6.0 to about +3.0. With attributes generated from one shared base (Player.cpp:3253-3281), a 3.0 steer still keeps the primary main on top via Step-1 pinning, but lets genuinely-high off-role fits compete for the non-reserved slots instead of being shut out. Update the explanatory comment at Player.cpp:570-576 to match.

STEP 4 — preserve one-tricks. For size<=2 players, SKIP the Step-2 cross-role reservation (set effective cross_role_min=0 when agent_pool_size<=2) so the ~6% one-trick band stays a pure same-role specialist. Gate the reservation: 'int kCross = (agent_pool_size <= 2) ? 0 : std::min(cross_role_min, agent_pool_size-1);'.

STEP 5 — tie flex/adaptability to cross-role breadth, not just size. At Player.cpp:1924 (FlexibleUtility) and the size roll, optionally set cross_role_min=3 for high-Adaptability or FlexibleUtility players so the league's designated flex players reliably cover 3 roles; leave default 2 for everyone else. Also revisit FlexGenius gate at Player.cpp:2303 (currently pool>=4) — with the new modal size 5 it will trigger far more often; confirm that is intended or raise its Adaptability threshold.

STEP 6 — verify with a distribution dump. Generate ~2000 players via generate_player and histogram (a) agent_pool.size() and (b) count of agents in agent_pool whose role != primary_role. Acceptance: median total pool 5, >=80% of non-one-trick players have >=2 off-role agents, one-trick (<=1 off-role) stays ~5-8%, ultra-flex (size 7) ~0.1-3%. The smoke_test.cpp harness (already references agent_pool) is the natural place to add this assertion.

FULL agent override path (Team.cpp:1266-1302): if agent_override has 5 named agents for this map, each agent is greedily matched to the best-rated player, with primary_role==agent.role getting a flat +25 fit bonus (Team.cpp:1285) and off-role players' contribution multiplied by 0.85 (Team.cpp:1292). Auto path step 1 (Team.cpp:1304-1311): decide_desired_tag picks a comp tag, comp_override may replace it; plan_for(desired_tag) yields a fixed CompPlan whose plan.need is a per-Role count. req (Team.cpp:1316-1319) is the exact list of roles the comp demands — this is the hard role budget.

Role assignment is a 3-phase fill into that fixed budget: Phase A (Team.cpp:1331-1337) gives each player their primary_role IF the plan still has that role slot free (take_role at Team.cpp:1325). Phase B (Team.cpp:1338-1349) flex players take the role of agent_pool.back() if that slot is free. Phase C (Team.cpp:1350-1358) leftovers get whatever role slot remains (else Duelist). Net effect: a player is almost always pinned to primary_role; they only move off-role when their role is over-subscribed on the roster vs the comp.

Per-player agent pick is HARD-LOCKED to the assigned role tr (Team.cpp:1366-1379): pool is first built ONLY from p->agent_pool entries whose a->role==tr (Team.cpp:1369-1371). Only if that is empty does off_role=true and the pool falls back to ALL agents of role tr from agents() (Team.cpp:1372-1375), then finally any unused agent (Team.cpp:1376-1378). So even a player whose pool contains a strong off-role agent can never be picked onto it unless their assigned role tr matches it.

Off-role penalty: best_off_role (Team.cpp:1393/1448) is true only when the player had NO pooled agent for their assigned role; rating is then multiplied by 0.85 (Team.cpp:1453-1454) before contributing to team_ovr. There is no graduated penalty keyed to actual role_fit_score — it is binary (in-pool-for-role vs not).

update_agent_pool (Player.cpp:562-646) already allows cross-role agents into agent_pool: Step 2 (Player.cpp:634-645) fills the pool with the next-highest scorers across ANY role (primary_role only gets a modest +6, Player.cpp:583), and the comment at Player.cpp:574-576 explicitly says a Duelist can get Cypher in their pool. So the off-role compatibility data already exists per player; build_round_selection just never uses it because the agent loop filters by a->role==tr.

Make off_role a graduated, per-candidate signal instead of a binary all-or-nothing pool flag. Today off_role is set once for the whole pool (Team.cpp:1368-1374) and best_off_role copies it (Team.cpp:1393/1448). Replace with a per-agent check inside the scoring loop (Team.cpp:1394-1450): compute `bool cand_off = (a->role != tr);` and when cand_off, scale that candidate's `score` by role_fit_score (e.g. multiply the rating_f*0.65 term, or add `(p->role_fit_score(a->role) - 1.0) * K`). Track best_off_role from the WINNING candidate, not the pool.

Replace the flat 0.85 team_ovr penalty (Team.cpp:1453-1454) with a fit-scaled penalty so a 'Good fit' off-role costs less than a 'Stretch': e.g. `if (best_off_role) rating *= (0.85 + 0.15 * p->role_fit_score(best_a->role));` — this keeps a true mismatch at ~0.85 but lets a high-compat off-role player keep ~95-100% of their rating, removing the disincentive that currently makes the auto picker avoid off-role slotting.

(Optional, for true role mobility) Loosen the role BUDGET in Phase B (Team.cpp:1338-1349) so a flex player can take a non-primary role even when their primary slot is still open, if role_fit_score for an open slot is high. Add a pre-pass that, for players whose agent_pool.front() or best off-role fit exceeds threshold, lets take_role try a high-fit non-primary role before Phase A locks everyone to primary. Keep behind the same kOffRoleThreshold to avoid scrambling comps.

Preserve invariants: keep the 'Duelist always present' guarantee — the comp req vector (Team.cpp:1316-1319) and Phase-A primary pinning ensure each demanded role is filled; only relax the AGENT chosen for a given role slot, or allow swaps that still satisfy plan.need. Verify the full-agent-override path (Team.cpp:1266-1302) is left intact since it already supports off-role and uses its own +25/0.85 logic.

Wire role_fit into the manual override too (optional consistency): the override path uses a flat +25 on-role bonus (Team.cpp:1285) and flat 0.85 off-role (Team.cpp:1292); switch the 0.85 there to the same fit-scaled formula for consistent off-role treatment across user and auto paths.

Build/verify with the project's build command from the Valosim memory, then run smoke_test.cpp (it references these symbols) and inspect a few build_round_selection results to confirm off-role compatible players now get slotted onto non-primary agents and team_ovr no longer collapses, while comps still fill every required role.

change_plan: Define one shared compatibility->multiplier helper so selection and resign share identical curve math. Add a free function (e.g. in Player.h or a small util, near role_fit_score's declaration ~Player.h:567): `inline double role_fit_penalty_mult(double rf){ if(rf>=1.0) return 1.0; double m = 0.78 + 0.22*rf; return m<0.78?0.78:(m>1.0?1.0:m); }` This maps rf=1.0->1.00 (no penalty), rf=0.80->0.956 (~4% haircut, the requested 'small penalty for 80%-compat Duelist->Sentinel'), rf=0.50->0.89, rf=0.30->0.846, rf=0.0->0.78 (worst ~22%). It is smooth/monotonic in rf and replaces the cliff at the single 0.85 threshold. (Floor 0.78 keeps a hard mismatch meaningfully worse than the old flat 0.85 without nuking the pick entirely.)

Then at Team.cpp:1453-1454 replace `double rating = best_rating_int; if (best_off_role) rating *= 0.85;` with `double rating = best_rating_int * role_fit_penalty_mult(best_fit);`. best_off_role / best_rating_int plumbing for the penalty can be dropped (best_off_role may still be kept if used elsewhere for UI, but it no longer drives the haircut).

current_behavior: STARTER SET IS PURELY POSITIONAL — there is NO is_starter flag. The starting 5 is literally roster[0..4] of the std::vector<PlayerPtr> Team::roster (include/Team.h:269). Bench = roster[5..6]. Roster is hard-capped at 7 (sign_player early-returns if roster.size()>=7, Team.cpp:417).

Player has NO starter/bench/order field (confirmed: grep of include/Player.h for is_starter|bench|starter_order|roster_slot|is_benched returns nothing). Benched simply means 'sits at vector index >=5'.

The match engine reads the first 5 vector slots directly: Team::build_round_selection slices std::vector<PlayerPtr> players(roster.begin(), roster.begin()+5) (Team.cpp:1313) and, for the user's full agent override, ps(roster.begin(), roster.begin()+5) (Team.cpp:1276). enforce_one_igl (Team.cpp:535) and enforce_one_flex (Team.cpp:657) both define starters = min(5, roster.size()) and only ever scan roster[0..4]. So IGL/Flex overlays are computed over whoever currently occupies the first 5 slots — reordering the vector automatically re-scopes them.

sign_player(Team.cpp:407/414) does roster.push_back(p) (always appends to the END — a 6th/7th signing lands on the bench by construction), clears promises, stamps exp_year, then calls enforce_one_igl()+enforce_one_flex() (Team.cpp:461-462). It does NOT reorder, so a new signing never auto-displaces a starter.

release_player(Team.cpp:711) does std::find + roster.erase(it) (Team.cpp:713-715) and ++cuts_this_year_. erase() shifts every later element left by one — so releasing a starter promotes whatever was at index 5 into the starting 5, and releasing shifts bench indices. NOTE: release_player does NOT call enforce_one_igl/flex afterward, unlike sign_player.

resign_player (Team.cpp:2338/2344) edits contract fields in place without touching vector order; market_value_estimate is a read-only FMV helper. None affect starter/bench status. Re-sign/Extend is explicitly allowed for bench + mid-contract players (user spec 2026-05-28, gui_main.cpp:5914-5945).

GUI DrawRoster DERIVES the buckets every frame — it does not store them. It walks roster[0..4], assigns the first occupant of each canonical Position (D/C/S/I) to canonical[idx], the next leftover starter to `fifth`, dup-collisions to `displaced`; bench = roster[5..] + displaced, then sorts bench by ovr() desc (gui_main.cpp:5772-5804). So the GUI 'bench' is a display projection of vector order + position-duplication, NOT persisted state.

Each roster row's only mutating actions today are Re-sign/Extend (opens negotiation modal, gui_main.cpp:5928-5944) and Release (pushes to to_release, applied via t->release_player at gui_main.cpp:6014-6017). There is NO bench/start/move-up/move-down control. The opponent Team Profile view mirrors the exact same derive-bucket logic (gui_main.cpp:8589-8612).

Team::ovr() averages the WHOLE roster, not just starters (Team.cpp:398-405) — benching does not change displayed team OVR. auto_fill_roster fills slots in canonical order 1D/2C/3I/4S/5flex only while roster.size()<5 (Team.cpp:851-867); the offseason GM pass adds at most one bench piece, max 6 total (Team.cpp:1772-...).

current_behavior: Happiness today is a SINGLE state: FreeAgentMood{ std::unordered_map<std::string,double> per_team; } at Player.h:286-288, instanced as `FreeAgentMood mood{};` at Player.h:422.
